hero_section.title:
I RUN COMPLEX TECH PRODUCTS BY DAY.
AND DISAPPEAR INTO MOUNTAINS, ISLANDS, AND UNTOLD PLACES BY NIGHT.
hero_section.subtitle: 5+ years shipping financial products across 60 countries — from APAC government ministries to global ERP ecosystems. High availability sustained by trekking endurance, island silence, and the clarity that only getting truly lost can give you.

journey_header.title: NOT A STRAIGHT LINE. THAT'S THE POINT.
journey_header.body: Started as delivery support. Grew into BA. Got promoted to PM by international clients. Appointed PO in outsourcing. Now owning a 60-country financial ecosystem at Mantu.

experience_timeline.role: Product Owner — Global Financial Management System · Cash & Working Capital
bullets: Own end-to-end Financial Execution Ecosystem across 60+ countries — Cash Advance → Expense → Billing → Payment → Accounting
Lead global e-invoicing for 20+ subsidiaries across Belgium, Portugal, Italy, China & Vietnam (Sovos, SEAM, Indicom, MISA, MagniFinance)
Designed complex API data flows and XML/JSON schemas ensuring 100% tax compliance across EU and Asian markets on a shared ERP
Architect central financial middleware distributing expense data from Expensya & TravelPerk to Kyriba payouts, client billing, and ERP GL entries
Operate across Europe, Latin America & Asia — daily collaboration with Finance, Treasury, Legal, and Engineering leaders

experience_timeline.role: Senior Business Analyst → BA Lead → Project Manager → Product Owner
bullets: Delivered 5+ long-term projects (6–12 months) across gov, marine, construction, and global automotive
Streamlined 50+ business workflows — up to 25% faster processing times
Promoted to PM by international client, led a 20-member team; BA Lead across three simultaneous projects
Appointed Product Owner — owned backlog and aligned product vision with client PMs
95% stakeholder satisfaction across all international clients
Delivered 2 end-to-end DX projects from requirements to UAT using Jira & Zoho Suite
Defined KPIs, calculation formulas, and business rules for performance tracking systems
Authored BRD, SRS, API mappings, ERD, and data dictionaries; built UI mockups in Figma
Conducted SQL queries and data analysis for reporting and integration requirements
Authored SRS, FRD and BPMN workflows across LMS, Healthcare, and HRM domains
Supported pre-sales requirements breakdown and UI/UX prototyping
Mapped customer journeys, contributed to UX improvement initiatives
Created training guides and end-user documentation; first exposure to product thinking
Led industry research and securities analysis — first contact with finance domain

case_studies.name: Global Financial Execution Ecosystem — Mantu
case_studies.problem: 60+ countries. 20+ subsidiaries. Fragmented e-invoicing across EU and Asia on a single shared ERP. Every country had different tax mandates, schemas, and compliance deadlines — with zero unified approach.
case_studies.what_i_did: End-to-end Cash & Working Capital pillar: Cash Advance → Expense Declaration → Billing → Payment → GL entries. Architected central financial middleware connecting Expensya, TravelPerk, Kyriba, and the ERP simultaneously. Designed API flows and XML/JSON schemas per country gateway.
case_studies.outcome: 100% tax compliance across all active markets. Multi-gateway e-invoicing live: Belgium/France (Sovos), China (SEAM), Italy (Indicom), Vietnam (MISA), Portugal (MagniFinance). ERP synchronization governing AR/AP lifecycle and audit traceability.

case_studies.name: Finance Management System — APAC Government Ministry
case_studies.problem: A government finance ministry ran invoices, payment vouchers, and approvals on paper. Slow, unauditable, zero traceability. NDA project.
case_studies.what_i_did: Collaborated with Singapore UX/UI squad and Vietnamese scrum teams. Mapped workflows via activity, DFD, state, and use case diagrams. Traveled onsite to Singapore for intensive discovery sessions with end users.
case_studies.outcome: Fully digitized core finance workflows on SharePoint. Paper chaos → auditable digital processes. Ministry stakeholders validated through structured UAT.

case_studies.name: Enterprise Digital Workspace — Post-Spinoff Company Build
chips: SAP S/4HANA · Workday · MS Power Platform
case_studies.problem: A Singaporean data center company had just split from its holding group. It needed to build a complete digital workspace from scratch — all departments, all processes, zero existing infrastructure.
case_studies.what_i_did: Led BA team to automate 40+ core processes across Finance, Procurement, Legal, Sales & HR. Facilitated technical investigations with SAP/Workday experts to define API and data mapping. Managed sprint delivery and BA resourcing across all workstreams simultaneously.
case_studies.outcome: 40+ processes centralized and integrated. Project extended 3 months with 5 additional resources — client confidence signal. Full Go-live and post-launch enhancement delivered.

case_studies.name: Sales Incentivization Portal — Global Automotive Brand
case_studies.problem: A top-tier automotive brand had no unified global platform to run sales competitions and incentive programs across multiple markets.
case_studies.what_i_did: Ran cross-regional requirement workshops. Translated business objectives into wireframes, functional specs, and acceptance criteria. Managed product backlog across 3 phased releases.
case_studies.outcome: Platform live across Korea, Turkey, Thailand. Agents earning and redeeming points under custom incentive schemes. Delivered iteratively in 3 phases.

about_section.quote: I have a business school degree, work with government ministries and 60-country ERPs daily, and I wrote my first BRD before I ever heard the word 'PO'. It all makes sense now.

operating_mode.title: MY OPERATING MODE
operating_mode.body: I sit at the table where business complexity meets engineering reality. I don't hand off requirements — I follow through to delivery. Having been BA, PM, and PO on the same projects, I understand what breaks at every transition point. That's what makes me different.

why_hybrid.title:
WHY HIRE
THE HYBRID
why_hybrid.body: Most BAs document. I translate. Most POs prioritize. I understand the regulatory why behind every priority. When you're shipping financial products for governments and global ERPs, that distinction matters enormously.
